---
title: "Real Income vs Housing Prices in Singapore"
subtitle: "Did income growth keep pace with HDB resale prices, 2000–2025?"
author:
  - name: Shina Shih Xin Rong (2402781)
  - name: Jeanie Cherie Chua Yue-Ning (2403083)
  - name: Claudia Yue Xin Ying (2401817)
  - name: Nurul Zahirah Binte Muhamadnoh (2401671)
  - name: Anushka Chourasia (2503769)
date: "2026-03-22"
format:
  html:
    embed-resources: true
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 2
    number-sections: false
    theme: cosmo
    include-in-header:
      text: |
        <style>
        table {
          border-top: none !important;
        }
        thead tr {
          border-top: none !important;
        }
        </style>
execute:
  echo: false
  warning: false
  message: false
---
```{r setup}
library(tidyverse)
library(knitr)
library(scales)

tidy_data <- readRDS("tidy_data.rds")

gap_data <- tidy_data %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = Measure_Type, values_from = Index_Value) %>%
  mutate(Affordability_Gap = `HDB Resale Price` - `Real Income`)

col_measure <- c("Real Income" = "#2166AC", "HDB Resale Price" = "#B2182B")
col_group   <- c("Bottom 20%" = "#D73027", "Middle 60%" = "#FC8D59", "Top 20%" = "#4575B4")

base_theme <- theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    plot.title       = element_text(face = "bold", size = 14, margin = margin(b = 6)),
    plot.subtitle    = element_text(size = 10, color = "grey40", margin = margin(b = 12)),
    plot.caption     = element_text(size = 8, color = "grey55", hjust = 0,
                                    margin = margin(t = 10)),
    plot.background  = element_rect(fill = "#FAFAFA", color = NA),
    panel.background = element_rect(fill = "#FAFAFA", color = NA),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.major = element_line(color = "grey90"),
    legend.position  = "bottom",
    legend.key.width = unit(1.5, "cm"),
    strip.text       = element_text(face = "bold", size = 12, margin = margin(b = 6)),
    axis.text        = element_text(color = "grey30"),
    axis.text.x      = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, size = 9)
  )

events <- tibble(
  xmin  = c(2008, 2020),
  xmax  = c(2009, 2021),
  label = c("GFC", "COVID-19")
)
```

## Original Visualisation

The original visualisation, *"Average Household Income Per Household vs Purchasing Power Index (PPI)"*, was sourced from Stacked and presents nominal household income across ten deciles alongside a general Purchasing Power Index on a dual axis, covering 2010 to 2020.

![Original visualisation: Average Household Income Per Household vs PPI](Average-Household-Income-Per-Household-Image.jpg){width=85% fig-align="center"}

## Weaknesses and Improvements

| # | Weakness in Original | Improvement in Our Visualisation |
|:--:|---|---|
| 1 | **Dual y-axis distortion.** Independently scaled axes create false impressions of correlation between income and PPI. | **Indexed comparison removes dual-axis distortion.** Both real income and HDB resale price series are converted to a common index rebased to 2010 = 100. All series share one y-axis and one scale, so any visual divergence reflects a genuine difference in growth rates. |
| 2 | **Ten income deciles.** Overlapping lines and an 11-entry legend cause visual congestion and reduce interpretability. | **Three aggregated income groups reduce cognitive load.** The ten deciles are consolidated into Bottom 20%, Middle 60%, and Top 20%, reducing the number of series from ten to four while preserving the distributional contrast relevant to the research question. |
| 3 | **Nominal income values.** Unadjusted for inflation, systematically overstating real income gains over time. | **Real income deflated by group-specific CPI.** Nominal income is deflated using the CPI series specific to each household income group, reflecting the reality that lower-income households face different price pressures than higher-income households. |
| 4 | **General Purchasing Power Index.** No link to housing costs, which represent the largest household expenditure in Singapore. | **HDB resale prices used as an explicit housing cost proxy.** The faceted layout places the HDB line identically across all three panels, so readers can directly compare how far housing costs have outpaced each income group without untangling crossing lines. |
| 5 | **Dense legend and high cognitive load.** Readers must constantly cross-reference between legend and chart to extract any insight. | **Shaded ribbon makes the affordability gap an explicit visual object.** The area between the income and HDB lines is filled as a shaded band, presenting the gap directly as a visible, named region rather than asking readers to mentally estimate the distance between two lines. |
| 6 | **No explicit affordability metric.** Readers must mentally estimate the distance between two independently scaled series. | **Direct endpoint labels replace axis-reading.** The 2025 index values for both series are annotated directly on each panel, making the key quantitative finding immediately legible without tracing values back to the y-axis. |
| 7 | **No economic context provided.** The original chart has no annotations marking key events that affected income or housing prices. | **Event annotations mark key economic periods.** The GFC (2008 to 2009) and COVID-19 (2020 to 2021) periods are marked with light vertical bands and clearly readable labels, allowing readers to connect the affordability trajectory to known economic events. |
: {tbl-colwidths="[4,48,48]"}

## The Improved Visualisation
```{r main-chart, fig.width=13, fig.height=6}
labels_2025 <- tidy_data %>%
  filter(Year == 2025) %>%
  mutate(label = paste0(round(Index_Value, 0)))

p <- ggplot(tidy_data,
            aes(x = Year, y = Index_Value, color = Measure_Type,
                linetype = Measure_Type)) +

  geom_ribbon(
    data = gap_data,
    aes(x = Year, ymin = `Real Income`, ymax = `HDB Resale Price`),
    inherit.aes = FALSE,
    fill = "#B2182B", alpha = 0.07
  ) +

  geom_rect(data = events,
            aes(xmin = xmin, xmax = xmax, ymin = -Inf, ymax = Inf),
            inherit.aes = FALSE,
            fill = "grey50", alpha = 0.10) +

  geom_text(data = events,
            aes(x = (xmin + xmax) / 2, y = 58, label = label),
            inherit.aes = FALSE,
            size = 2.5, color = "grey35", fontface = "italic",
            angle = 90) +

  geom_hline(yintercept = 100, linetype = "dashed",
             color = "grey55", linewidth = 0.45) +

  geom_line(linewidth = 1.1) +
  geom_point(data = tidy_data %>% filter(Year %% 5 == 0),
             size = 2.2, shape = 21, fill = "white", stroke = 1.1) +

  geom_text(
    data = labels_2025,
    aes(label = label),
    hjust = -0.2, size = 3.2, fontface = "bold",
    show.legend = FALSE
  ) +

  facet_wrap(~Income_Group, ncol = 3) +

  scale_color_manual(values = col_measure, name = NULL) +
  scale_linetype_manual(values = c("Real Income"      = "solid",
                                   "HDB Resale Price" = "solid"),
                        name = NULL) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(2000, 2025, by = 5),
                     expand = expansion(mult = c(0.02, 0.14))) +
  scale_y_continuous(
    breaks = seq(60, 180, by = 20),
    limits = c(55, 185),
    sec.axis = sec_axis(~ ., breaks = 100,
                        labels = "← base year (2010 = 100)")
  ) +

  labs(
    title    = "Real Household Income vs HDB Resale Price Index (2010 = 100)",
    subtitle = paste0(
      "Singapore, 2000–2025  |  Income deflated by group-specific CPI  |  ",
      "Shaded band = affordability gap  |  Labels show 2025 index values"
    ),
    x       = NULL,
    y       = "Index value (2010 = 100)",
    caption = paste0(
      "Sources: Department of Statistics Singapore — Average Monthly Household Employment Income by Decile; ",
      "CPI by Household Income Group (2024 base year).\n",
      "Housing and Development Board / data.gov.sg — HDB Resale Flat Prices (transaction records, 2000–2025).\n",
      "Note: Income groups deflated using their respective group-specific CPI. ",
      "HDB index computed as annual median resale price, rebased to 2010 = 100."
    )
  ) +

  base_theme +
  theme(
    legend.text        = element_text(size = 11),
    legend.spacing.x   = unit(0.4, "cm"),
    axis.text.y.right  = element_text(size = 8, color = "grey50",
                                      face = "italic", hjust = 0),
    axis.ticks.y.right = element_line(color = "grey55")
  )

p
```

**Reading the chart.** Each panel shows one income group. The blue line tracks real (inflation-adjusted) household income; the red line tracks HDB resale prices. Both are indexed to 2010 = 100, so the lines share a common baseline. The shaded band between the lines is the affordability gap: when red rises above blue, housing is outpacing income.

---

## Key Insights

**Before 2010**, real income broadly kept pace with or exceeded HDB resale prices across all three income groups, reflecting relative affordability stability in the early 2000s.

**From 2010 onward**, resale prices diverge upward. The gap widens progressively, then accelerates sharply after 2020, coinciding with the post-COVID resale surge driven by demand brought forward from the pandemic period.

**By 2025**, the HDB index reaches 171.6, its highest level in the 25-year window, while real income indices are 140.5 (Bottom 20%), 141.8 (Middle 60%), and 113.8 (Top 20%). The affordability gap is largest in absolute index-point terms for the Top 20% (57.8 points), but this reflects real income plateauing under CPI deflation rather than greater financial stress. **The Bottom 20% gap (31.1 points) is the most policy-relevant**: their lower absolute income provides far less buffer against rising prices, and their real income growth in 2020 to 2025 is the weakest of all three groups.

---

## Supporting Analysis

::: {.callout-note collapse="true"}
### Affordability gap over time

Before 2010, all three income groups show negative gaps, meaning real income growth was outpacing HDB resale prices. From 2010 onward, gaps turn persistently positive and widen sharply after 2020. By 2025, the Top 20% shows the largest index-point gap (+57.8 pts), though this reflects CPI deflation eroding their nominal gains rather than greater financial hardship. The Bottom 20% gap (+31.1 pts) and Middle 60% gap (+29.8 pts) are more policy-relevant given their lower absolute income buffers.
```{r gap-chart, fig.width=10, fig.height=5.5}
gap_labels_2025 <- gap_data %>%
  filter(Year == 2025) %>%
  mutate(
    label = paste0(Income_Group, ": +", round(Affordability_Gap, 1), " pts"),
    y_pos = case_when(
      Income_Group == "Top 20%"    ~ 59,
      Income_Group == "Bottom 20%" ~ 32,
      Income_Group == "Middle 60%" ~ 26
    )
  )

ggplot(gap_data, aes(x = Year, y = Affordability_Gap, color = Income_Group)) +
  geom_rect(data = events,
            aes(xmin = xmin, xmax = xmax, ymin = -Inf, ymax = Inf),
            inherit.aes = FALSE, fill = "grey50", alpha = 0.10) +
  geom_text(data = events,
            aes(x = (xmin + xmax) / 2, y = -39, label = label),
            inherit.aes = FALSE,
            size = 2.5, color = "grey35", fontface = "italic", angle = 90) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "grey50", linewidth = 0.5) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1.1) +
  geom_point(size = 1.8) +
  geom_text(
    data = gap_labels_2025,
    aes(y = y_pos, label = label),
    x = 2026, hjust = 0, size = 3, fontface = "bold",
    show.legend = FALSE
  ) +
  scale_color_manual(values = col_group, name = NULL) +
  scale_x_continuous(
    breaks = seq(2000, 2025, by = 5),
    limits = c(1999, 2033),
    expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0))
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(
    breaks = seq(-40, 80, by = 20),
    limits = c(-42, 85)
  ) +
  labs(
    title    = "Affordability gap by income group",
    subtitle = "Gap = HDB index minus Real income index  |  Positive = housing outpacing income",
    x = NULL, y = "Gap (index points)",
    caption  = "Sources: SingStat, HDB / data.gov.sg"
  ) +
  base_theme
```
:::

::: {.callout-note collapse="true"}
### CAGR by sub-period

In the 2000 to 2010 period, HDB price growth (4.3% per year) already outpaced real income growth across all groups, with the Bottom 20% recording a slightly negative real income CAGR (-0.1%). The 2010 to 2020 period saw income growth recover slightly while HDB price growth moderated (1.5% per year). The 2020 to 2025 period is the most damaging: HDB prices surged at 8.1% per year across all groups while real income growth slowed, with the Top 20% recording a negative real income CAGR (-0.5%), directly explaining the widening gaps seen above.
```{r cagr-chart, fig.width=11, fig.height=5}
cagr_fn <- function(end_val, start_val, n) round(((end_val / start_val)^(1 / n) - 1) * 100, 2)

periods <- list(c(2000, 2010), c(2010, 2020), c(2020, 2025))

income_cagr <- map_dfr(periods, function(p) {
  tidy_data %>%
    filter(Measure_Type == "Real Income", Year %in% p) %>%
    pivot_wider(names_from = Year, values_from = Index_Value) %>%
    mutate(
      CAGR       = cagr_fn(.[[4]], .[[3]], p[2] - p[1]),
      Sub_Period = paste0(p[1], " to ", p[2]),
      Measure    = "Real Income"
    ) %>%
    select(Income_Group, Sub_Period, Measure, CAGR)
})

hdb_cagr <- map_dfr(periods, function(p) {
  vals <- tidy_data %>%
    filter(Measure_Type == "HDB Resale Price", Year %in% p) %>%
    distinct(Year, Index_Value) %>%
    pivot_wider(names_from = Year, values_from = Index_Value)
  tibble(
    CAGR       = cagr_fn(vals[[2]], vals[[1]], p[2] - p[1]),
    Sub_Period = paste0(p[1], " to ", p[2]),
    Measure    = "HDB Resale Price"
  )
}) %>%
  crossing(Income_Group = c("Bottom 20%", "Middle 60%", "Top 20%"))

cagr_data <- bind_rows(income_cagr, hdb_cagr) %>%
  mutate(
    Income_Group = factor(Income_Group, levels = c("Bottom 20%", "Middle 60%", "Top 20%")),
    Sub_Period   = factor(Sub_Period,   levels = c("2000 to 2010", "2010 to 2020", "2020 to 2025")),
    Measure      = factor(Measure,      levels = c("Real Income", "HDB Resale Price"))
  )

ggplot(cagr_data, aes(x = Sub_Period, y = CAGR, fill = Measure)) +
  geom_col(position = position_dodge(0.65), width = 0.55) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linewidth = 0.4, color = "grey40") +
  geom_text(
    aes(label = paste0(round(CAGR, 1), "%"),
        vjust = ifelse(CAGR >= 0, -0.4, 1.3)),
    position = position_dodge(0.65),
    size = 2.8, fontface = "bold"
  ) +
  facet_wrap(~Income_Group, ncol = 3) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = col_measure, name = NULL) +
  scale_y_continuous(
    labels = function(x) paste0(x, "%"),
    breaks = seq(-2, 10, by = 2)
  ) +
  labs(
    title    = "CAGR of real income vs HDB resale prices by sub-period",
    subtitle = "Three periods: 2000 to 2010, 2010 to 2020, 2020 to 2025",
    x = NULL, y = "CAGR (%)",
    caption  = "Sources: SingStat, HDB / data.gov.sg"
  ) +
  base_theme +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 0, hjust = 0.5))
```
:::
